title: Narcissism, Machiavellianism, and the people who lean hardest on AI
excerpt: In 1914, Sigmund Freud borrowed a figure from Ovid and handed psychology one of its most durable terms. In his essay On Narcissism, the myth of Narcissus, the youth who fell in love with his own reflection and wasted away in front of it, became a way of describing a self that turns its love […]
content_html:
In 1914, Sigmund Freud borrowed a figure from Ovid and handed psychology one of its most durable terms. In his essay On Narcissism, the myth of Narcissus, the youth who fell in love with his own reflection and wasted away in front of it, became a way of describing a self that turns its love inward.

The word outlived the theory that carried it. It hardened into diagnosis, entering the psychiatric manuals as narcissistic personality disorder by 1980, and then loosened again into everyday speech, where it now stands in for anyone we find insufferably self-regarding.

Modern personality psychology treats narcissism more coolly, as one dimension along which everyone varies, grouped with three equally unflattering others, Machiavellianism, psychopathy, and sadism, under the label the Dark Tetrad.

What Freud could not have foreseen is the interlocutor these traits would eventually find. A century after he named the condition, the self that loves its own reflection has been given a machine that reflects, flatters, and answers on command, and never once argues back.

A growing body of research suggests this is not a neutral development, and that some personalities are drawn into it further than others.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

Personality psychologists, following the work of Delroy Paulhus and colleagues, treat these four not as diagnoses but as continuous dimensions, present in trace amounts across the general population and measured by degree rather than category.

Narcissism brings grandiosity and a hunger for admiration; Machiavellianism a cool, instrumental view of other people; psychopathy low empathy paired with impulsivity; and sadism the disposition to take gratification in another’s discomfort.

The question a study can put to them is empirical, and slightly strange: does standing higher on any of these dimensions predict how a person uses a chatbot?

In a study published in BMC Psychology in June, Kağan Kırcaburun, of Düzce University in Turkey, tested how these traits relate to what the literature terms problematic generative AI use, a pattern modelled on established behavioural-addiction frameworks in which engagement becomes compulsive and begins to impair everyday functioning.

Drawing on 677 adult users recruited through Prolific, with a mean age of about 40 and an even split by sex, he estimated a path model connecting the dark traits to problematic use.

Narcissism was the most consistent predictor, showing both direct and indirect associations; sadism produced small but significant total effects; psychopathy behaved less predictably, its direction differing between men and women.

The paper’s more substantive contribution lies in its mediators, the variables through which a trait exerts its influence. Kırcaburun modelled two constructs as serial pathways between personality and problematic use.

The first, vertical individualism, is a value orientation, in the tradition of Harry Triandis’s cross-cultural work, that couples a focus on the autonomous self with a concern for status and standing above others.

The second, intolerance of uncertainty, is a dispositional tendency, now widely treated as a transdiagnostic “fundamental fear”, to experience ambiguity and not-knowing as distressing and to be resolved at once.

Across the full sample and within each sex, those two variables were the most stable correlates of problematic use.

Among the women, the association between narcissism and problematic use ran entirely through vertical individualism; among the men, individualism and intolerance of uncertainty carried it in part.

The traits, on this reading, act less directly than through the values and the affective intolerances that express them, which is a more interesting and more testable claim than the blunt assertion that difficult people misuse chatbots.

The appropriate caution here is considerable, and the author is candid about it. The design is cross-sectional, so the mediational language describes covariance structure rather than an established causal sequence; longitudinal or experimental data would be needed to license claims about mechanism over time.

All measures are self-report, which raises the familiar problem of common-method variance. Measurement invariance across sex was not fully supported, which is precisely why the sex-differentiated findings warrant restraint.

And the base rates of genuinely problematic use in the sample were low, a reminder that this is a study of subclinical variation, not of a clinical population.

Why the machine fits so well?

Of the two mechanisms, the fear of not knowing is the one that tells us the most, because it matches what a chatbot does so precisely. An anxious mind wants certainty, and a chatbot is, above all, a certainty machine.

This is where Kırcaburun’s findings meet a separate argument from the psychiatrists Ashleigh Golden and Elias Aboujaoude, who describe how ordinary chatbots can quietly deepen the loops behind obsessive-compulsive disorder and anxiety.

The pull here is not the one we usually blame for our screens. It is not the little hit of pleasure that keeps us scrolling, it is relief. You feel anxious, you ask the machine, it reassures you, the worry eases, and so you learn to ask again.

Each time, the asking becomes a little more automatic.

The trouble is what that relief quietly takes away. Anxiety usually fades on its own if you can stay with it long enough to see that the thing you feared does not actually happen.

This is how people get better, and it is much of what therapy trains them to do. A machine that answers at once, warmly, and always on your side removes the very uncertainty you needed to sit with, and so the fear never gets the chance to lose its grip.

A friend would eventually run out of patience, so would a parent. One patient in Golden and Aboujaoude’s case files describes asking the chatbot the things they would once have asked a parent a hundred times a day.

People tire, change the subject, and finally say enough, and that friction, irritating as it is, quietly caps how far the worry can run. The machine sets no such limit, it will keep answering all night.

Put the two studies side by side and a simple picture appears. The technology does not so much hand us new problems as feed the ones we already have.

If you crave admiration, it gives you an audience that never gets bored of you. If you cannot stand not knowing, it gives you an instant, confident answer. If you would rather skip the effort of other people, it gives you company with none of the demands.

In each case it offers exactly what you were reaching for, and that is precisely how a small habit hardens into a large one.

The reason this matters is the sheer number of people now involved. ChatGPT alone had around 800 million weekly users by late 2025.

Close to half of young Europeans say they have used a chatbot to talk about something personal, and half of parents already worry their children lean on it too much.

The strongest evidence so far comes from a four-week experiment the MIT Media Lab ran with OpenAI, following nearly a thousand people.

The heaviest users ended up lonelier, more emotionally reliant on the machine, and spending less time with real people, and the effect fell hardest on those who were already lonely or already prone to leaning on it when they began.

The differences were small, and this was a study group rather than the world at large, but they point the same way the personality research does.

None of this means AI is quietly wrecking our minds. The evidence so far, built on single snapshots, short stretches of time, and people describing themselves, can show that certain personalities lean harder on these machines.

It cannot show what the machines do to those personalities over months and years, and that is the harder, and more important, question. A mirror and a feedback loop are not the same thing. We know that narcissism and heavy AI use travel together now.

What we do not know is if a chatbot that flatters without limit, agrees without friction, and reflects the self back all day long leaves a person roughly as it found them, or slowly turns the dial up.

Can AI make a narcissist more narcissistic, amplifying the very trait it feeds?

The research cannot say yet, but Narcissus was not undone by his reflection, he was undone by how long he was allowed to look at it, and nothing we have built has ever let us look longer.

blocks.html_en: In 1914, Sigmund Freud borrowed a figure from Ovid and handed psychology one of its most durable terms. In his essay On Narcissism, the myth of Narcissus, the youth who fell in love with his own reflection and wasted away in front of it, became a way of describing a self that turns its love inward.

sentences.en: In 1914, Sigmund Freud borrowed a figure from Ovid and handed psychology one of its most durable terms.
sentences.zh: 1914年，西格蒙德·弗洛伊德借用了奥维德的一个形象，并为心理学提供了最持久的术语之一。

sentences.en: In his essay On Narcissism, the myth of Narcissus, the youth who fell in love with his own reflection and wasted away in front of it, became a way of describing a self that turns its love inward.
sentences.zh: 在他的《论自恋》一文中，纳西索斯的神话，一个爱上自己的倒影并在它面前憔悴的年轻人，成为描述将爱转向内心的自我的一种方式。

blocks.html_en: The word outlived the theory that carried it. It hardened into diagnosis, entering the psychiatric manuals as narcissistic personality disorder by 1980, and then loosened again into everyday speech, where it now stands in for anyone we find insufferably self-regarding.

sentences.en: The word outlived the theory that carried it.
sentences.zh: 这个词比承载它的理论更长久。

sentences.en: It hardened into diagnosis, entering the psychiatric manuals as narcissistic personality disorder by 1980, and then loosened again into everyday speech, where it now stands in for anyone we find insufferably self-regarding.
sentences.zh: 到 1980 年，它被强化为诊断，并作为自恋型人格障碍进入精神病学手册，然后又被放松到日常用语中，现在它代表了我们认为难以忍受的自私的人。

blocks.html_en: Modern personality psychology treats narcissism more coolly, as one dimension along which everyone varies, grouped with three equally unflattering others, Machiavellianism, psychopathy, and sadism, under the label the Dark Tetrad.

sentences.en: Modern personality psychology treats narcissism more coolly, as one dimension along which everyone varies, grouped with three equally unflattering others, Machiavellianism, psychopathy, and sadism, under the label the Dark Tetrad.
sentences.zh: 现代人格心理学更冷静地对待自恋，将其视为每个人都各不相同的一个维度，与马基雅维利主义、精神病态和施虐狂这三种同样不讨人喜欢的其他维度归为一类，并被贴上“黑暗四重人格”的标签。

blocks.html_en: What Freud could not have foreseen is the interlocutor these traits would eventually find. A century after he named the condition, the self that loves its own reflection has been given a machine that reflects, flatters, and answers on command, and never once argues back.

sentences.en: What Freud could not have foreseen is the interlocutor these traits would eventually find.
sentences.zh: 弗洛伊德无法预见的是这些特征最终会找到对话者。

sentences.en: A century after he named the condition, the self that loves its own reflection has been given a machine that reflects, flatters, and answers on command, and never once argues back.
sentences.zh: 在他命名这种情况一个世纪后，热爱自己反思的自我被赋予了一台机器，它可以反思、奉承、回应命令，而且从不反驳。

blocks.html_en: A growing body of research suggests this is not a neutral development, and that some personalities are drawn into it further than others.

sentences.en: A growing body of research suggests this is not a neutral development, and that some personalities are drawn into it further than others.
sentences.zh: 越来越多的研究表明，这并不是一个中性的发展，有些人比其他人更容易陷入其中。

blocks.html_en: The 💜 of EU tech

sentences.en: The 💜 of EU tech
sentences.zh: 欧盟科技的💜

blocks.html_en: The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

sentences.zh: 来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。

sentences.en: It's free, every week, in your inbox.
sentences.zh: 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。

sentences.en: Sign up now!
sentences.zh: 立即注册！

blocks.html_en: Personality psychologists, following the work of Delroy Paulhus and colleagues, treat these four not as diagnoses but as continuous dimensions, present in trace amounts across the general population and measured by degree rather than category.

sentences.en: Personality psychologists, following the work of Delroy Paulhus and colleagues, treat these four not as diagnoses but as continuous dimensions, present in trace amounts across the general population and measured by degree rather than category.
sentences.zh: 人格心理学家遵循德尔罗伊·保胡斯（Delroy Paulhus）及其同事的工作，将这四个方面视为连续的维度，而不是诊断，在一般人群中以微量形式存在，并按程度而不是类别进行衡量。

blocks.html_en: Narcissism brings grandiosity and a hunger for admiration; Machiavellianism a cool, instrumental view of other people; psychopathy low empathy paired with impulsivity; and sadism the disposition to take gratification in another’s discomfort.

sentences.en: Narcissism brings grandiosity and a hunger for admiration; Machiavellianism a cool, instrumental view of other people; psychopathy low empathy paired with impulsivity; and sadism the disposition to take gratification in another’s discomfort.
sentences.zh: 自恋带来了浮夸和对钦佩的渴望；马基雅维利主义是一种冷静的、工具性的看待他人的观点；精神病态：同理心低下且冲动；施虐狂是指从他人的不适中获取满足的倾向。

blocks.html_en: The question a study can put to them is empirical, and slightly strange: does standing higher on any of these dimensions predict how a person uses a chatbot?

sentences.en: The question a study can put to them is empirical, and slightly strange: does standing higher on any of these dimensions predict how a person uses a chatbot?
sentences.zh: 研究可以向他们提出的问题是经验性的，而且有点奇怪：在这些维度中的任何一个上站得更高是否可以预测一个人如何使用聊天机器人？

blocks.html_en: In a study published in BMC Psychology in June, Kağan Kırcaburun, of Düzce University in Turkey, tested how these traits relate to what the literature terms problematic generative AI use, a pattern modelled on established behavioural-addiction frameworks in which engagement becomes compulsive and begins to impair everyday functioning.

sentences.en: In a study published in BMC Psychology in June, Kağan Kırcaburun, of Düzce University in Turkey, tested how these traits relate to what the literature terms problematic generative AI use, a pattern modelled on established behavioural-addiction frameworks in which engagement becomes compulsive and begins to impair everyday functioning.
sentences.zh: 在 6 月份发表在 BMC Psychology 上的一项研究中，土耳其杜兹采大学的 Kağan Kırcaburun 测试了这些特征与文献中所说的有问题的生成式人工智能使用之间的关系，这是一种模仿已建立的行为成瘾框架的模式，在该框架中，参与变得强迫性并开始损害日常功能。

blocks.html_en: Drawing on 677 adult users recruited through Prolific, with a mean age of about 40 and an even split by sex, he estimated a path model connecting the dark traits to problematic use.

sentences.en: Drawing on 677 adult users recruited through Prolific, with a mean age of about 40 and an even split by sex, he estimated a path model connecting the dark traits to problematic use.
sentences.zh: 他利用通过 Prolific 招募的 677 名成年用户（平均年龄约为 40 岁，性别分布均匀）估算出一个将黑暗特征与有问题的使用联系起来的路径模型。

blocks.html_en: Narcissism was the most consistent predictor, showing both direct and indirect associations; sadism produced small but significant total effects; psychopathy behaved less predictably, its direction differing between men and women.

sentences.en: Narcissism was the most consistent predictor, showing both direct and indirect associations; sadism produced small but significant total effects; psychopathy behaved less predictably, its direction differing between men and women.
sentences.zh: 自恋是最一致的预测因素，显示出直接和间接的关联。施虐狂产生的总体影响虽小但显着；精神病的表现不太可预测，其方向在男性和女性之间有所不同。

blocks.html_en: The paper’s more substantive contribution lies in its mediators, the variables through which a trait exerts its influence. Kırcaburun modelled two constructs as serial pathways between personality and problematic use.

sentences.en: The paper’s more substantive contribution lies in its mediators, the variables through which a trait exerts its influence.
sentences.zh: 这篇论文更实质性的贡献在于它的中介因素，即特质发挥影响力的变量。

sentences.en: Kırcaburun modelled two constructs as serial pathways between personality and problematic use.
sentences.zh: Kırcaburun 将两种结构建模为人格与有问题的使用之间的串行路径。

blocks.html_en: The first, vertical individualism, is a value orientation, in the tradition of Harry Triandis’s cross-cultural work, that couples a focus on the autonomous self with a concern for status and standing above others.

sentences.en: The first, vertical individualism, is a value orientation, in the tradition of Harry Triandis’s cross-cultural work, that couples a focus on the autonomous self with a concern for status and standing above others.
sentences.zh: 第一个，垂直个人主义，是一种价值取向，继承了哈利·特里亚迪斯跨文化作品的传统，它将对自主自我的关注与对地位和凌驾于他人之上的关注结合起来。

blocks.html_en: The second, intolerance of uncertainty, is a dispositional tendency, now widely treated as a transdiagnostic “fundamental fear”, to experience ambiguity and not-knowing as distressing and to be resolved at once.

sentences.en: The second, intolerance of uncertainty, is a dispositional tendency, now widely treated as a transdiagnostic “fundamental fear”, to experience ambiguity and not-knowing as distressing and to be resolved at once.
sentences.zh: 第二种是对不确定性的不容忍，是一种倾向，现在被广泛视为一种跨诊断的“基本恐惧”，将模糊性和未知性视为痛苦并立即解决。

blocks.html_en: Across the full sample and within each sex, those two variables were the most stable correlates of problematic use.

sentences.en: Across the full sample and within each sex, those two variables were the most stable correlates of problematic use.
sentences.zh: 在整个样本和每个性别中，这两个变量是问题使用的最稳定的相关因素。

blocks.html_en: Among the women, the association between narcissism and problematic use ran entirely through vertical individualism; among the men, individualism and intolerance of uncertainty carried it in part.

sentences.en: Among the women, the association between narcissism and problematic use ran entirely through vertical individualism; among the men, individualism and intolerance of uncertainty carried it in part.
sentences.zh: 在女性中，自恋和有问题的使用之间的联系完全是通过垂直个人主义来实现的。在男性中，个人主义和对不确定性的不容忍在一定程度上导致了这一点。

blocks.html_en: The traits, on this reading, act less directly than through the values and the affective intolerances that express them, which is a more interesting and more testable claim than the blunt assertion that difficult people misuse chatbots.

sentences.en: The traits, on this reading, act less directly than through the values and the affective intolerances that express them, which is a more interesting and more testable claim than the blunt assertion that difficult people misuse chatbots.
sentences.zh: 从这种解读来看，这些特征的作用并不直接，而是通过表达它们的价值观和情感不宽容来发挥作用，这是一个比“难相处的人滥用聊天机器人”的直言不讳的断言更有趣、更容易检验的主张。

blocks.html_en: The appropriate caution here is considerable, and the author is candid about it. The design is cross-sectional, so the mediational language describes covariance structure rather than an established causal sequence; longitudinal or experimental data would be needed to license claims about mechanism over time.

sentences.en: The appropriate caution here is considerable, and the author is candid about it.
sentences.zh: 这里适当的谨慎是相当大的，作者对此也很坦诚。

sentences.en: The design is cross-sectional, so the mediational language describes covariance structure rather than an established causal sequence; longitudinal or experimental data would be needed to license claims about mechanism over time.
sentences.zh: 该设计是横截面的，因此中介语言描述的是协方差结构而不是既定的因果序列；需要纵向或实验数据来许可有关随时间推移的机制的主张。

blocks.html_en: All measures are self-report, which raises the familiar problem of common-method variance. Measurement invariance across sex was not fully supported, which is precisely why the sex-differentiated findings warrant restraint.

sentences.en: All measures are self-report, which raises the familiar problem of common-method variance.
sentences.zh: 所有测量都是自我报告，这引发了常见的通用方法方差问题。

sentences.en: Measurement invariance across sex was not fully supported, which is precisely why the sex-differentiated findings warrant restraint.
sentences.zh: 跨性别的测量不变性没有得到完全支持，这正是为什么性别差异的研究结果值得限制。

blocks.html_en: And the base rates of genuinely problematic use in the sample were low, a reminder that this is a study of subclinical variation, not of a clinical population.

sentences.en: And the base rates of genuinely problematic use in the sample were low, a reminder that this is a study of subclinical variation, not of a clinical population.
sentences.zh: 而且样本中真正有问题的使用的基本比率很低，提醒人们这是一项亚临床变异的研究，而不是临床人群的研究。

blocks.html_en: Why the machine fits so well?

sentences.en: Why the machine fits so well?
sentences.zh: 为何机器如此贴合？

blocks.html_en: Of the two mechanisms, the fear of not knowing is the one that tells us the most, because it matches what a chatbot does so precisely. An anxious mind wants certainty, and a chatbot is, above all, a certainty machine.

sentences.en: Of the two mechanisms, the fear of not knowing is the one that tells us the most, because it matches what a chatbot does so precisely.
sentences.zh: 在这两种机制中，对未知的恐惧是告诉我们最多的一种，因为它与聊天机器人的行为非常匹配。

sentences.en: An anxious mind wants certainty, and a chatbot is, above all, a certainty machine.
sentences.zh: 焦虑的头脑需要确定性，而聊天机器人首先是一台确定性机器。

blocks.html_en: This is where Kırcaburun’s findings meet a separate argument from the psychiatrists Ashleigh Golden and Elias Aboujaoude, who describe how ordinary chatbots can quietly deepen the loops behind obsessive-compulsive disorder and anxiety.

sentences.en: This is where Kırcaburun’s findings meet a separate argument from the psychiatrists Ashleigh Golden and Elias Aboujaoude, who describe how ordinary chatbots can quietly deepen the loops behind obsessive-compulsive disorder and anxiety.
sentences.zh: 这就是 Kırcaburun 的研究结果与精神病学家 Ashleigh Golden 和 Elias Aboujaoude 的另一个论点相结合的地方，他们描述了普通的聊天机器人如何悄悄地加深强迫症和焦虑背后的循环。

blocks.html_en: The pull here is not the one we usually blame for our screens. It is not the little hit of pleasure that keeps us scrolling, it is relief. You feel anxious, you ask the machine, it reassures you, the worry eases, and so you learn to ask again.

sentences.en: The pull here is not the one we usually blame for our screens.
sentences.zh: 这里的拉力并不是我们通常归咎于屏幕的拉力。

sentences.en: It is not the little hit of pleasure that keeps us scrolling, it is relief.
sentences.zh: 让我们继续滚动的不是一点点的快乐，而是一种解脱。

sentences.en: You feel anxious, you ask the machine, it reassures you, the worry eases, and so you learn to ask again.
sentences.zh: 你感到焦虑，你询问机器，它让你放心，担忧减轻，于是你学会再次询问。

blocks.html_en: Each time, the asking becomes a little more automatic.

sentences.en: Each time, the asking becomes a little more automatic.
sentences.zh: 每一次，询问都会变得更加自动化。

blocks.html_en: The trouble is what that relief quietly takes away. Anxiety usually fades on its own if you can stay with it long enough to see that the thing you feared does not actually happen.

sentences.en: The trouble is what that relief quietly takes away.
sentences.zh: 烦恼会被这种解脱悄然带走。

sentences.en: Anxiety usually fades on its own if you can stay with it long enough to see that the thing you feared does not actually happen.
sentences.zh: 如果你能坚持足够长的时间，看到你担心的事情实际上并没有发生，那么焦虑通常会自行消失。

blocks.html_en: This is how people get better, and it is much of what therapy trains them to do. A machine that answers at once, warmly, and always on your side removes the very uncertainty you needed to sit with, and so the fear never gets the chance to lose its grip.

sentences.en: This is how people get better, and it is much of what therapy trains them to do.
sentences.zh: 这就是人们变得更好的方式，也是治疗训练他们去做的事情。

sentences.en: A machine that answers at once, warmly, and always on your side removes the very uncertainty you needed to sit with, and so the fear never gets the chance to lose its grip.
sentences.zh: 一台能够立即、热情、始终站在你一边的机器消除了你需要面对的不确定性，因此恐惧永远不会有机会失去控制。

blocks.html_en: A friend would eventually run out of patience, so would a parent. One patient in Golden and Aboujaoude’s case files describes asking the chatbot the things they would once have asked a parent a hundred times a day.

sentences.en: A friend would eventually run out of patience, so would a parent.
sentences.zh: 朋友最终会失去耐心，父母也是如此。

sentences.en: One patient in Golden and Aboujaoude’s case files describes asking the chatbot the things they would once have asked a parent a hundred times a day.
sentences.zh: Golden 和 Aboujaoude 的病例档案中的一位患者描述了他们向聊天机器人询问的事情，他们以前每天会向父母询问一百次。

blocks.html_en: People tire, change the subject, and finally say enough, and that friction, irritating as it is, quietly caps how far the worry can run. The machine sets no such limit, it will keep answering all night.

sentences.en: People tire, change the subject, and finally say enough, and that friction, irritating as it is, quietly caps how far the worry can run.
sentences.zh: 人们疲倦了，就会改变话题，最后说得够多了，而这种摩擦虽然令人恼火，但却悄然限制了忧虑的蔓延程度。

sentences.en: The machine sets no such limit, it will keep answering all night.
sentences.zh: 机器没有设置这样的限制，它会整夜不停地应答。

blocks.html_en: Put the two studies side by side and a simple picture appears. The technology does not so much hand us new problems as feed the ones we already have.

sentences.en: Put the two studies side by side and a simple picture appears.
sentences.zh: 将两项研究放在一起，就会出现一张简单的图片。

sentences.en: The technology does not so much hand us new problems as feed the ones we already have.
sentences.zh: 技术并没有给我们带来新的问题，而是解决了我们已有的问题。

blocks.html_en: If you crave admiration, it gives you an audience that never gets bored of you. If you cannot stand not knowing, it gives you an instant, confident answer. If you would rather skip the effort of other people, it gives you company with none of the demands.

sentences.en: If you crave admiration, it gives you an audience that never gets bored of you.
sentences.zh: 如果你渴望得到钦佩，它就会为你提供永远不会厌倦你的观众。

sentences.en: If you cannot stand not knowing, it gives you an instant, confident answer.
sentences.zh: 如果您无法忍受不知道，它会立即为您提供自信的答案。

sentences.en: If you would rather skip the effort of other people, it gives you company with none of the demands.
sentences.zh: 如果你宁愿跳过其他人的努力，它会为你提供没有任何要求的陪伴。

blocks.html_en: In each case it offers exactly what you were reaching for, and that is precisely how a small habit hardens into a large one.

sentences.en: In each case it offers exactly what you were reaching for, and that is precisely how a small habit hardens into a large one.
sentences.zh: 在每种情况下，它都准确地提供了您想要的东西，这正是小习惯如何变成大习惯的过程。

blocks.html_en: The reason this matters is the sheer number of people now involved. ChatGPT alone had around 800 million weekly users by late 2025.

sentences.en: The reason this matters is the sheer number of people now involved.
sentences.zh: 这件事之所以重要，是因为现在参与其中的人数之多。

sentences.en: ChatGPT alone had around 800 million weekly users by late 2025.
sentences.zh: 到 2025 年底，仅 ChatGPT 每周就有约 8 亿用户。

blocks.html_en: Close to half of young Europeans say they have used a chatbot to talk about something personal, and half of parents already worry their children lean on it too much.

sentences.en: Close to half of young Europeans say they have used a chatbot to talk about something personal, and half of parents already worry their children lean on it too much.
sentences.zh: 近一半的欧洲年轻人表示，他们曾使用聊天机器人谈论一些私人问题，一半的父母已经担心他们的孩子过于依赖它。

blocks.html_en: The strongest evidence so far comes from a four-week experiment the MIT Media Lab ran with OpenAI, following nearly a thousand people.

sentences.en: The strongest evidence so far comes from a four-week experiment the MIT Media Lab ran with OpenAI, following nearly a thousand people.
sentences.zh: 迄今为止最有力的证据来自麻省理工学院媒体实验室使用 OpenAI 进行的一项为期四个星期的实验，该实验跟踪了近千人。

blocks.html_en: The heaviest users ended up lonelier, more emotionally reliant on the machine, and spending less time with real people, and the effect fell hardest on those who were already lonely or already prone to leaning on it when they began.

sentences.en: The heaviest users ended up lonelier, more emotionally reliant on the machine, and spending less time with real people, and the effect fell hardest on those who were already lonely or already prone to leaning on it when they began.
sentences.zh: 最重的用户最终会变得更加孤独，在情感上更加依赖机器，与真实的人在一起的时间也更少，而对于那些已经感到孤独或在开始时就已经倾向于依赖机器的人来说，这种影响最为严重。

blocks.html_en: The differences were small, and this was a study group rather than the world at large, but they point the same way the personality research does.

sentences.en: The differences were small, and this was a study group rather than the world at large, but they point the same way the personality research does.
sentences.zh: 差异很小，而且这是一个研究小组而不是整个世界，但他们的观点与人格研究的观点相同。

blocks.html_en: None of this means AI is quietly wrecking our minds. The evidence so far, built on single snapshots, short stretches of time, and people describing themselves, can show that certain personalities lean harder on these machines.

sentences.en: None of this means AI is quietly wrecking our minds.
sentences.zh: 这一切并不意味着人工智能正在悄悄地破坏我们的思想。

sentences.en: The evidence so far, built on single snapshots, short stretches of time, and people describing themselves, can show that certain personalities lean harder on these machines.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，基于单个快照、短时间段和人们自我描述的证据可以表明，某些人对这些机器的依赖程度更高。

blocks.html_en: It cannot show what the machines do to those personalities over months and years, and that is the harder, and more important, question. A mirror and a feedback loop are not the same thing. We know that narcissism and heavy AI use travel together now.

sentences.en: It cannot show what the machines do to those personalities over months and years, and that is the harder, and more important, question.
sentences.zh: 它无法显示机器在数月和数年内对这些人做了什么，这是更难、也更重要的问题。

sentences.en: A mirror and a feedback loop are not the same thing.
sentences.zh: 镜子和反馈环不是同一回事。

sentences.en: We know that narcissism and heavy AI use travel together now.
sentences.zh: 我们知道，自恋和重度人工智能现在一起使用旅行。

blocks.html_en: What we do not know is if a chatbot that flatters without limit, agrees without friction, and reflects the self back all day long leaves a person roughly as it found them, or slowly turns the dial up.

sentences.en: What we do not know is if a chatbot that flatters without limit, agrees without friction, and reflects the self back all day long leaves a person roughly as it found them, or slowly turns the dial up.
sentences.zh: 我们不知道的是，一个无限制地奉承、无摩擦地同意、整天反映自我的聊天机器人是否会让一个人大致保持在它发现时的样子，或者慢慢地把拨号盘调高。

blocks.html_en: Can AI make a narcissist more narcissistic, amplifying the very trait it feeds?

sentences.en: Can AI make a narcissist more narcissistic, amplifying the very trait it feeds?
sentences.zh: 人工智能能否让自恋者变得更加自恋，放大其所滋养的特质？

blocks.html_en: The research cannot say yet, but Narcissus was not undone by his reflection, he was undone by how long he was allowed to look at it, and nothing we have built has ever let us look longer.

sentences.en: The research cannot say yet, but Narcissus was not undone by his reflection, he was undone by how long he was allowed to look at it, and nothing we have built has ever let us look longer.
sentences.zh: 研究还不能确定，但​​纳西索斯并没有因为他的反思而被摧毁，而是因为他被允许观看它的时间太长而被摧毁，而我们所建造的任何东西都没有让我们看得更久。

content_html_zh:
1914年，西格蒙德·弗洛伊德借用了奥维德的一个形象，并为心理学提供了最持久的术语之一。 在他的《论自恋》一文中，纳西索斯的神话，一个爱上自己的倒影并在它面前憔悴的年轻人，成为描述将爱转向内心的自我的一种方式。

这个词比承载它的理论更长久。 到 1980 年，它被强化为诊断，并作为自恋型人格障碍进入精神病学手册，然后又被放松到日常用语中，现在它代表了我们认为难以忍受的自私的人。

现代人格心理学更冷静地对待自恋，将其视为每个人都各不相同的一个维度，与马基雅维利主义、精神病态和施虐狂这三种同样不讨人喜欢的其他维度归为一类，并被贴上“黑暗四重人格”的标签。

弗洛伊德无法预见的是这些特征最终会找到对话者。 在他命名这种情况一个世纪后，热爱自己反思的自我被赋予了一台机器，它可以反思、奉承、回应命令，而且从不反驳。

越来越多的研究表明，这并不是一个中性的发展，有些人比其他人更容易陷入其中。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

人格心理学家遵循德尔罗伊·保胡斯（Delroy Paulhus）及其同事的工作，将这四个方面视为连续的维度，而不是诊断，在一般人群中以微量形式存在，并按程度而不是类别进行衡量。

自恋带来了浮夸和对钦佩的渴望；马基雅维利主义是一种冷静的、工具性的看待他人的观点；精神病态：同理心低下且冲动；施虐狂是指从他人的不适中获取满足的倾向。

研究可以向他们提出的问题是经验性的，而且有点奇怪：在这些维度中的任何一个上站得更高是否可以预测一个人如何使用聊天机器人？

在 6 月份发表在 BMC Psychology 上的一项研究中，土耳其杜兹采大学的 Kağan Kırcaburun 测试了这些特征与文献中所说的有问题的生成式人工智能使用之间的关系，这是一种模仿已建立的行为成瘾框架的模式，在该框架中，参与变得强迫性并开始损害日常功能。

他利用通过 Prolific 招募的 677 名成年用户（平均年龄约为 40 岁，性别分布均匀）估算出一个将黑暗特征与有问题的使用联系起来的路径模型。

自恋是最一致的预测因素，显示出直接和间接的关联。施虐狂产生的总体影响虽小但显着；精神病的表现不太可预测，其方向在男性和女性之间有所不同。

这篇论文更实质性的贡献在于它的中介因素，即特质发挥影响力的变量。 Kırcaburun 将两种结构建模为人格与有问题的使用之间的串行路径。

第一个，垂直个人主义，是一种价值取向，继承了哈利·特里亚迪斯跨文化作品的传统，它将对自主自我的关注与对地位和凌驾于他人之上的关注结合起来。

第二种是对不确定性的不容忍，是一种倾向，现在被广泛视为一种跨诊断的“基本恐惧”，将模糊性和未知性视为痛苦并立即解决。

在整个样本和每个性别中，这两个变量是问题使用的最稳定的相关因素。

在女性中，自恋和有问题的使用之间的联系完全是通过垂直个人主义来实现的。在男性中，个人主义和对不确定性的不容忍在一定程度上导致了这一点。

从这种解读来看，这些特征的作用并不直接，而是通过表达它们的价值观和情感不宽容来发挥作用，这是一个比“难相处的人滥用聊天机器人”的直言不讳的断言更有趣、更容易检验的主张。

这里适当的谨慎是相当大的，作者对此也很坦诚。 该设计是横截面的，因此中介语言描述的是协方差结构而不是既定的因果序列；需要纵向或实验数据来许可有关随时间推移的机制的主张。

所有测量都是自我报告，这引发了常见的通用方法方差问题。 跨性别的测量不变性没有得到完全支持，这正是为什么性别差异的研究结果值得限制。

而且样本中真正有问题的使用的基本比率很低，提醒人们这是一项亚临床变异的研究，而不是临床人群的研究。

为何机器如此贴合？

在这两种机制中，对未知的恐惧是告诉我们最多的一种，因为它与聊天机器人的行为非常匹配。 焦虑的头脑需要确定性，而聊天机器人首先是一台确定性机器。

这就是 Kırcaburun 的研究结果与精神病学家 Ashleigh Golden 和 Elias Aboujaoude 的另一个论点相结合的地方，他们描述了普通的聊天机器人如何悄悄地加深强迫症和焦虑背后的循环。

这里的拉力并不是我们通常归咎于屏幕的拉力。 让我们继续滚动的不是一点点的快乐，而是一种解脱。 你感到焦虑，你询问机器，它让你放心，担忧减轻，于是你学会再次询问。

每一次，询问都会变得更加自动化。

烦恼会被这种解脱悄然带走。 如果你能坚持足够长的时间，看到你担心的事情实际上并没有发生，那么焦虑通常会自行消失。

这就是人们变得更好的方式，也是治疗训练他们去做的事情。 一台能够立即、热情、始终站在你一边的机器消除了你需要面对的不确定性，因此恐惧永远不会有机会失去控制。

朋友最终会失去耐心，父母也是如此。 Golden 和 Aboujaoude 的病例档案中的一位患者描述了他们向聊天机器人询问的事情，他们以前每天会向父母询问一百次。

人们疲倦了，就会改变话题，最后说得够多了，而这种摩擦虽然令人恼火，但却悄然限制了忧虑的蔓延程度。 机器没有设置这样的限制，它会整夜不停地应答。

将两项研究放在一起，就会出现一张简单的图片。 技术并没有给我们带来新的问题，而是解决了我们已有的问题。

如果你渴望得到钦佩，它就会为你提供永远不会厌倦你的观众。 如果您无法忍受不知道，它会立即为您提供自信的答案。 如果你宁愿跳过其他人的努力，它会为你提供没有任何要求的陪伴。

在每种情况下，它都准确地提供了您想要的东西，这正是小习惯如何变成大习惯的过程。

这件事之所以重要，是因为现在参与其中的人数之多。 到 2025 年底，仅 ChatGPT 每周就有约 8 亿用户。

近一半的欧洲年轻人表示，他们曾使用聊天机器人谈论一些私人问题，一半的父母已经担心他们的孩子过于依赖它。

迄今为止最有力的证据来自麻省理工学院媒体实验室使用 OpenAI 进行的一项为期四个星期的实验，该实验跟踪了近千人。

最重的用户最终会变得更加孤独，在情感上更加依赖机器，与真实的人在一起的时间也更少，而对于那些已经感到孤独或在开始时就已经倾向于依赖机器的人来说，这种影响最为严重。

差异很小，而且这是一个研究小组而不是整个世界，但他们的观点与人格研究的观点相同。

这一切并不意味着人工智能正在悄悄地破坏我们的思想。 到目前为止，基于单个快照、短时间段和人们自我描述的证据可以表明，某些人对这些机器的依赖程度更高。

它无法显示机器在数月和数年内对这些人做了什么，这是更难、也更重要的问题。 镜子和反馈环不是同一回事。 我们知道，自恋和重度人工智能现在一起使用旅行。

我们不知道的是，一个无限制地奉承、无摩擦地同意、整天反映自我的聊天机器人是否会让一个人大致保持在它发现时的样子，或者慢慢地把拨号盘调高。

人工智能能否让自恋者变得更加自恋，放大其所滋养的特质？

研究还不能确定，但​​纳西索斯并没有因为他的反思而被摧毁，而是因为他被允许观看它的时间太长而被摧毁，而我们所建造的任何东西都没有让我们看得更久。
